content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as recursive self-improving agents, where the memory module itself acts as an independent curiosity-driven agent that actively selects which new agent interaction traces to encode, prune, or retrieve instead of being a passive storage layer for the main task agent. This creates a nested agent architecture where the memory system autonomously optimizes its own content to improve the main agent's downstream performance.
